chapters.title: Why AI can feel so natural
chapters.text:
When the menu is too long, choosing is hard. With ChatGPT, the catalog of prompts is so large that it is easy to lose the thread when you need a task done. The ChatGPT Playground, another GPT-3 engine, offers a wider, more professional set of features than the standard chat. Right now, it is the most advanced and at the same time the simplest, friendliest platform for real-time results.

To use the ChatGPT Playground, you need an OpenAI account. Since you already know how to set one up, the next move is to pick the ChatGPT Playground from the list of engines in the top right corner. After that, the flow is the same as before. You get a reply to your clear, detailed prompt. It looks simple, and yet sometimes the results come back muddled. There are extra features, though, that you can use to sharpen your first input. Some of the most requested cues are answering questions, fixing grammar, simplifying complex texts, translating between languages, turning natural language into computer code and back again, explaining code in plain terms, squashing code bugs, building tables and lists from long passages, classifying tweets by sentiment, pulling keywords out of long texts, generating names, and creating spreadsheets.

The list goes on, but it helps to know the actual scope of what ChatGPT can do so you can shape effective prompts. Whatever your request, the main task is to stay precise and use the essential details.

Children learn from playgrounds, and adults can borrow that playful approach to master new technologies.

Extra prompts are not the only way to improve the output. The other two ways are switching to another GPT-3 engine and tuning the temperature of the results. The temperature setting changes how narrow or broad the outcomes are, depending on the task. GPT-3 offers four different engines. They are DaVinci, Curie, Babbage, and Ada.

DaVinci became the default engine out of the four because of how well it handled complex and creative questions. Even though ChatGPT is free, it still charges small amounts for use. Unlike DaVinci, the other three engines cost less and reply faster.

Curie does a better job at translating, answering direct questions, and excerpting and organizing technical information. Babbage, on the other hand, is better at generating material when you give it enough input. As for Ada, this engine shines at creative tasks in a way the others do not.

Finally, tuning the temperature between zero and one is the last way to land the most accurate results. A setting closer to one gives more random, creative outcomes, while a setting of zero gives more calculated outputs.

chapters.title: What exactly is ChatGPT?
chapters.text:
It can feel as if ChatGPT dropped from the heavens one Sunday morning. Some people expect it to lift their workload, while others worry about losing their jobs. The truth is that ChatGPT has been around for much longer, just out of sight of the public.

The history of ChatGPT, which stands for Generative Pre-trained Transformer, started in 2018 when a promising research lab called OpenAI produced its first engine. Backed early on by Microsoft, the lab built an engine to process natural speech from millions of users. With access to huge text datasets, the first GPT learned to write coherent passages in the style of how we speak, publish our news, and write our books. The next two versions, GPT-2 and GPT-3, sharpened their grasp of language and produced more fluent text. ChatGPT entered the chat in 2021 as an offshoot of GPT-3, developed specifically for everyday language. The new engine lets you use ChatGPT in chat boxes or emails. So far, it is the most refined language engine around, with a wide set of features.

It can generate text for storytelling and content creation, translate between languages, answer questions with relevant and coherent replies, hold human-like chats that consider the previous context, draft email responses, and analyze mood to make sure the text sounds positive or neutral.

The big difference between ChatGPT and its earlier versions is sharper context and better accuracy. That improvement makes it more and more valuable in language-related fields. Unlike the first language engines that worked with limited data sets, ChatGPT inherited a much broader and more flexible database to grow from. More complex neural networks let this engine adjust to huge amounts of information and use them in practical ways.

ChatGPT mirrors our communication with each other. Its bias is our bias, and its blind spots are ours too.

Another key feature, the transformer-based design, helps ChatGPT analyze bigger chunks of data at the same time. As a result, the engine improves faster and more efficiently than traditional ones. That design also helps it focus on several parts of a text and connect them with the context in mind. In practice, it makes ChatGPT a useful engine for machine translation tools, chatbots, and other related technologies.

On the other hand, OpenAI is upfront about using people's conversations with ChatGPT to improve the service. You can ask to erase your information and to flag inaccuracies. ChatGPT is free, which lets you explore modern technologies without limits.

Microsoft owns forty-nine percent of OpenAI's shares.

chapters.title: Learning to speak fluent ChatGPT
chapters.text:
Following the buzz around ChatGPT, many people have already played with it. The promise of solving a tough homework problem or writing a wedding vow in the voice of Shakespeare is hard to resist. And why would it not be tempting when registration takes less than five minutes?

If you have ever signed up for a social network, ChatGPT is already within reach. The basic registration steps include opening chat.openai.com and clicking the Signup button, typing in your email and choosing a secure password, confirming your email through the link in your inbox, filling in the requested personal information, and confirming your phone number as the final step. You can also sign up with your Google or Microsoft account. You will find all the options in the very first window.

After you sign in, you can start a conversation with ChatGPT on any topic. To get a decent result, though, you need to follow a few rules. Give attention to the context, because ChatGPT gives a higher-quality reply to a focused request. Know the limitations, because ChatGPT can produce untrue or unrelated results, so check the outcome carefully. Watch for ethical issues, because an offensive output is always possible, and you can report such incidents.

ChatGPT is incredibly limited, but good enough at some things to create a misleading impression of greatness, as Sam Altman has said.

Even with those drawbacks, ChatGPT can do a lot of useful things, like writing programming code, scripting videos, or crafting ads for your business. Good input guarantees efficient output, which means you need to be able to give clear prompts. A good cue is clear, specific, and relevant. For example, asking it to write an ad for my small business is too vague. What kind of business do you run, what is your target market, and what product details matter?

Once you have spelled out those points, you can ask it to write an ad for a small business that sells homemade honey and wax to local households, emphasizing the advantages of natural honey and the lower price compared to the market. That prompt does not waste words confusing ChatGPT, so the result is more useful. If you do not like the offered ad, you can reset the prompt by clicking the Try Again button. ChatGPT will craft another version you might prefer.

Smart AI starts with the smart user.

chapters.title: Getting the most out of ChatGPT
chapters.text:
When the menu is too long, choosing is tricky. With ChatGPT, the sheer number of prompts is so large that you can lose the thread when you need work done. The ChatGPT Playground, another GPT-3 engine, offers a wider, more professional set of features than the standard chat. Right now, it is the most advanced and also the simplest, friendliest platform for real-time results.

To use the ChatGPT Playground, you need an OpenAI account. Since you already know how to set one up, the next move is to pick the ChatGPT Playground from the list of engines in the top right corner. chapters.title: Becoming employee of the month with ChatGPT
chapters.text:
Finally, we have all gathered to answer the question of how ChatGPT can make you more productive. The range of services has shown that ChatGPT is useful in many situations, but the same variety can frustrate some users. The engine can help with personal tasks and with business tasks. It also plays well with certain programs and offers a few extensions a professional can lean on.

The most requested use of ChatGPT is text generation, including reports and emails, which many jobs need. The engine offers two texting modes. They are Conditional and Unconditional. The first mode uses a specific cue and context to produce a result. Asking for the capital of Spain is an example of the conditional mode. Other instances include completing the prompt or sentence, answering questions, building dialogues, summarizing large pieces of text, paraphrasing, translating languages, and classifying texts.

The unconditional mode does not need as much context or a particular cue. ChatGPT can give a final result without further supervision. This mode works best for creating fiction and novels, composing poetry and song lyrics, producing blog posts and news articles, writing social media posts, and programming.

ChatGPT picks up new knowledge from the diverse data on the internet to write creative pieces. Unfortunately, the booming demand for ChatGPT created a problem for servers that could not keep up. The quick fix was to launch Chrome extensions. It allowed for more niche versatility and took some pressure off the servers.

The most popular ChatGPT extensions target different tasks. WebChatGPT pulls in information that was unavailable to ChatGPT, especially anything after 2021. ChatGPT Writer generates emails and messages that you can copy to any website. ChatGPT for Google and Merlin produce side-by-side results with Google Chrome. LINER highlights sentences, images, and videos regardless of the source's format. TweetGPT creates Twitter posts directly without you having to enter ChatGPT. YouTube Summary with ChatGPT pulls the main points from a video transcript. ChatGPT Prompt Genius offers more cues when ChatGPT is exhausted. Promptheus turns ChatGPT into a voice assistant. Numerous.ai gives you more formulas for Google Sheets to solve more tasks.

ChatGPT helps you boost your productivity and, on top of that, can help you build another income stream. A few ways to do that are creating a website around any interest or goal, starting a copywriting career, writing YouTube scripts and headlines, and putting together a competitive CV.

ChatGPT lets you start a career without the fear of failure.

chapters.title: ChatGPT and the red code for humanity
chapters.text:
It is hard to ignore the elephant in the room. We are afraid of artificial intelligence even as it helps us. The fact that we cannot always tell human and AI-produced speech apart stirs a quiet existential panic. Even with all the benefits, we cannot look the other way on this ethical question. But was there an elephant in the first place?

ChatGPT is not perfect, especially in OpenAI's eyes. Hateful comments, cheating, and the engine's unstoppable learning make users uneasy. We tend to forget, though, that this language engine springs from people's minds. A team of brilliant programmers poured the current knowledge into the language engine, and the engine uses it to learn. In other words, ChatGPT does not generate its own expertise. It reflects ours as human beings. Negative results and bias are the AI echoing how we communicate with each other. To put it another way, we are the problem, not AI.

We grant AI something we treasure and fear the most, which is our values.

Another reason you do not need to be afraid of ChatGPT is that it does not have its own capabilities. Many AI alternatives use the same database and offer better service, and university AI tools are good examples. They have to be advanced to help students learn better. Companies like Microsoft and Google are also working on their own AI tools, which means competition will lead to technical improvement.

Because ChatGPT struggles with a huge flood of users, you may find yourself staring at a frozen screen trying to fix it. Some ways to deal with that are to exit ChatGPT to relieve the servers, check the server's capacity at Downdetector, clear your browsing data like cache and cookies, turn off all extensions and VPN connections, switch to Incognito mode, upgrade your network driver, restart your network settings, switch to a different browser, and report the problem.

Even with many ways to fix the issue, the way it keeps coming back may push you to look for alternatives. Luckily, before ChatGPT's popularity took off, many AI engines were already in use. Next time there is an issue, you can try Google Bard, Microsoft Bing, Chatsonic, Jasper Chat, Character AI, You.com, DialoGPT, Perplexity AI, Replika, and Neeva AI.

It is worth reminding ourselves that AI is safe for us as long as we are responsible for the quality of data we feed it.

AI language engines can perpetuate biases and stereotypes, and human validation can ensure that the content is ethical and free of bias, as Arsath Natheem has said.

chapters.title: Final thoughts
chapters.text:
Artificial intelligence is slowly becoming an indispensable part of our lives, no matter where we live or what time zone we are in. The arrival of ChatGPT, however, kicked off a revolution. An advanced language engine, it appeared like a magic wand for office workers on deadlines and for sleepless students. Back in 2018, a private research lab launched the first version of what we now know as ChatGPT. Capable of writing texts, answering questions, and even translating computer code, this engine can make life easier for everyone, for free.

On the other hand, it does not remove the need for the human mind. To get the best output, the user has to provide good input. Knowing which formula or extension to use is critical to the final result, which means collaboration between humans and AI will remain necessary. ChatGPT is not our enemy. It is more like a student. That means our responsibility is bigger than we thought. As AI learns from our online interactions, social media posts, and comments, we have to change the way we communicate and treat each other. Solving our social problems can keep AI on our side, turning it into an assistant rather than a threat.

Building that responsibility as ChatGPT users takes attention. No AI engine is perfect. They make mistakes and deliver wrong results. We have to rely on ourselves even as the world goes more digital, which means education is always relevant.

Denying the growing impact of artificial intelligence is like fighting a windmill. Instead of opposing progress, we can learn to be smarter with technology so we stay at the same pace as AI while helping it keep up with us.

Try this. Train yourself regularly to use ChatGPT for your work. Notice issues and report them. Keep your social media hygiene. Comment and post responsibly. Do not make ChatGPT your primary source of information, and do your own research as well.
